annotations.label: Local ring (equivalent definitions)

anchor.snippet: A ring R is a local ring if it has any one of the following equivalent properties

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing` is defined via the equivalent condition that for any a+b=1, one of a or b is a unit.

annotations.label: Maximal ideals coincide with Jacobson radical

anchor.snippet: the unique maximal left ideal coincides with the unique maximal right ideal

annotations.note: In a commutative local ring the Jacobson radical equals the maximal ideal.

annotations.label: Commutative local ring characterization

anchor.snippet: a commutative ring is local if and only if it has a unique maximal ideal

annotations.note: Combined with `IsLocalRing.maximal_ideal_unique` this gives the iff characterization.

annotations.label: Local domain

anchor.snippet: A local ring that is an integral domain is called a local domain

annotations.note: Not a distinct concept in Mathlib; expressible by combining `[IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R]`.

annotations.label: Fields are local rings

anchor.snippet: All fields (and skew fields ) are local rings

annotations.note: Every field is a local ring via the anonymous instance in `Field` namespace.

annotations.label: Z/p^n is local

anchor.snippet: is a local ring ( p prime, n ≥ 1 )

annotations.note: No specific `IsLocalRing (ZMod (p^n))` instance was found.

annotations.label: Ring with units and nilpotents is local

anchor.snippet: a nonzero ring in which every element is either a unit or nilpotent is a local ring

annotations.note: The exact statement was not found, but `of_nonunits_add` proves local from closure of nonunits under addition (which nilpotents satisfy).

annotations.label: Discrete valuation rings

anchor.snippet: An important class of local rings are discrete valuation rings

annotations.note: DVRs are formalized as `IsDiscreteValuationRing`, defined as a local PID that is not a field.

annotations.label: Formal power series ring is local

anchor.snippet: whose elements are infinite series

annotations.note: Instance shows `IsLocalRing (MvPowerSeries σ R)` whenever `R` is local; specializes to F[[X]].

annotations.label: Formal power series over a local ring is local

anchor.snippet: every ring of formal power series over a local ring is local

annotations.note: The instance `[IsLocalRing R] : IsLocalRing (MvPowerSeries σ R)` is exactly this.

annotations.label: Dual numbers and F[X]/(X^n) are local

anchor.snippet: the algebra of dual numbers over any field is local

annotations.note: Dual numbers are formalized as local (instance in DualNumber), but F[X]/(X^n) does not have an explicit `IsLocalRing` instance.

annotations.label: Quotients of local rings are local

anchor.snippet: Nonzero quotient rings of local rings are local

annotations.note: Any surjective image (hence any nonzero quotient) of a local ring is local via `of_surjective'`.

annotations.label: Rationals with odd denominator

anchor.snippet: The ring of rational numbers with odd denominator is local

annotations.note: This specific ring Z_(2) is not defined as a named object; it would arise as `Localization.AtPrime (2)`.

annotations.label: Localization at a prime ideal

anchor.snippet: the localization of R at P is local

annotations.note: The instance shows `IsLocalRing (Localization P.primeCompl)` for any prime ideal P.

annotations.label: Polynomial ring is not local

anchor.snippet: is not local, since

annotations.note: No specific `¬ IsLocalRing (Polynomial R)` lemma; general non-local infrastructure exists in `NonLocalRing.lean`.

annotations.label: Integers are not local

anchor.snippet: is not local since it has a maximal ideal

annotations.note: No specific `¬ IsLocalRing ℤ` lemma was found.

annotations.label: Z/(pq) is not local

anchor.snippet: where p and q are distinct prime numbers

annotations.note: No specific lemma about ZMod (p*q) with distinct primes not being local.

annotations.label: Germs of continuous functions

anchor.snippet: the equivalence classes are what are called the

annotations.note: Mathlib has germs (`Germ`) and stalks but not a specific ring-of-germs-of-continuous-functions construction.

annotations.label: Invertible germ characterization

anchor.snippet: we need to characterize its invertible elements

annotations.note: No formalization of the ring of germs of continuous functions or invertibility characterization there.

annotations.label: Ring of germs is local

anchor.snippet: the sum of any two non-invertible germs is again non-invertible

annotations.note: The ring of germs of continuous functions is not set up as an `IsLocalRing` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rings of germs in various settings

anchor.snippet: Exactly the same arguments work for the ring of germs of continuous real-valued functions

annotations.note: No formalized ring-of-germs-of-continuous/smooth/holomorphic-functions instances of `IsLocalRing`.

annotations.label: Valuation ring

anchor.snippet: a valuation ring of a field K is a subring R such that for every non-zero element

annotations.note: `ValuationRing` is the Mathlib predicate for valuation rings.

annotations.label: Valuation rings are local

anchor.snippet: Any such subring will be a local ring

annotations.note: The instance `ValuationRing.isLocalRing` gives `IsLocalRing A` for a valuation ring `A`.

annotations.label: Endomorphism ring local iff indecomposable

anchor.snippet: if the endomorphism ring of the module M is local, then M is indecomposable

annotations.note: No lemma tying local endomorphism ring to module indecomposability was found.

annotations.label: Finite length indecomposable module has local endomorphism ring

anchor.snippet: if the module M has finite length and is indecomposable, then its endomorphism ring is local

annotations.note: Fitting's lemma for endomorphism rings of finite-length indecomposable modules is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group algebra kG of a p-group is local

anchor.snippet: then the group algebra kG is local

annotations.note: No `IsLocalRing (MonoidAlgebra k G)` instance for p-group over char-p field was found.

annotations.label: m-adic topology

anchor.snippet: This is the m -adic topology on R

annotations.note: `Ideal.adicTopology` gives the I-adic topology for any ideal; specializing to the maximal ideal gives the m-adic topology.

annotations.label: Krull's intersection theorem

anchor.snippet: Krull's intersection theorem

annotations.note: Krull's intersection theorem for Noetherian local rings: `⨅ n, I^n = ⊥` for `I ≠ ⊤`.

annotations.label: Germs of smooth functions show Noetherian assumption is crucial

anchor.snippet: let R be the ring of germs of infinitely differentiable functions at 0

annotations.note: The ring of germs of smooth functions and its non-Noetherian local structure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cohen structure theorem

anchor.snippet: Complete Noetherian local rings are classified by the Cohen structure theorem

annotations.note: The Cohen structure theorem for complete Noetherian local rings is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Residue field

anchor.snippet: is called the residue field of the local ring or residue field of the point P

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing.ResidueField R := R ⧸ maximalIdeal R`, with a Field instance.

annotations.label: Local ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: a local ring homomorphism from R to S is a ring homomorphism

annotations.note: `IsLocalHom` predicate; used with `IsLocalRing` throughout the RingHom subfolder.

annotations.label: Local ring homomorphisms are exactly continuous ring homomorphisms

anchor.snippet: These are precisely the ring homomorphisms that are continuous with respect to the given topologies

annotations.note: The characterization of local ring hom as continuous for m-adic topologies was not found.

annotations.label: Jacobson radical equals non-units

anchor.snippet: consists precisely of the non-units of the ring

annotations.note: Combining `IsLocalRing.mem_maximalIdeal` (maximal ideal = nonunits) with `ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal` yields the statement, but no single lemma stated as Jacobson = nonunits.

annotations.label: Jacobson radical is the unique maximal two-sided ideal

anchor.snippet: it is the unique maximal two-sided ideal of R

annotations.note: In a commutative local ring the maximal ideal is unique; matches the two-sided statement in the commutative setting.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for invertibility

anchor.snippet: For an element x of the local ring R , the following are equivalent

annotations.note: `x ∉ maximalIdeal R ↔ IsUnit x` captures the invertibility characterization.

annotations.label: Factor ring R/m is a skew field

anchor.snippet: the factor ring R / m is a skew field

annotations.note: The Field instance on `ResidueField R = R ⧸ maximalIdeal R` gives the (commutative) skew-field statement.

annotations.label: Quotient by two-sided ideal is local

anchor.snippet: the factor ring R / J is again local

annotations.note: Applying `of_surjective'` to `Ideal.Quotient.mk` (as done in Quotient.lean) yields the local quotient.

annotations.label: Kaplansky: projective modules over local rings are free

anchor.snippet: A deep theorem by Irving Kaplansky says that any projective module over a local ring is free

annotations.note: Kaplansky's theorem that projective modules over local rings are free is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Morita equivalence classifies rings Morita equivalent to local rings

anchor.snippet: the only rings Morita equivalent to a local ring R are (isomorphic to) the matrix rings over R

annotations.note: Morita equivalence and matrix rings exist in Mathlib but the classification of rings Morita equivalent to a local ring is not formalized.
